Strategy 3.name: property_attributed_income_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Taxable property income attributed to non-filers from national accounts
Strategy 3.logic: Taxable property income of employees who are not required to submit an annual tax report. Amount from national accounts Row n.22 (in million NIS). Distributed using quantile matching: a reference group (tax_report=1, wages_s2>0, property_reported_income_s3>0) establishes the relationship between wage quantiles and property income. For the target group (tax_report=0, wages_s2>0), each individual's wage percentile is mapped to a property income value via linear interpolation on the reference distribution, with min/max extrapolation for out-of-range percentiles. Negative predictions are floored at zero, and all predictions are scaled so their sum equals the national accounts total.
Strategy 3.code:
def create_property_attributed_income(df, total_amount):
    result_df = df.copy()
    result_df["property_attributed_income_s3"] = 0
    mask_target = (result_df["tax_report"] == 0) & (result_df["wages_s2"] > 0)
    mask_ref = (
        (result_df["tax_report"] == 1)
        & (result_df["wages_s2"] > 0)
        & (result_df["property_reported_income_s3"] > 0)
    )
    ref_wages = result_df.loc[mask_ref, "wages_s2"].values
    ref_property = result_df.loc[mask_ref, "property_reported_income_s3"].values
    target_wages = result_df.loc[mask_target, "wages_s2"].values
    idx = np.argsort(ref_wages)
    sorted_wages = ref_wages[idx]
    sorted_property = ref_property[idx]
    ref_pct = np.linspace(0, 1, len(sorted_wages))
    target_pct = stats.rankdata(target_wages, method="average") / len(target_wages)
    f_interp = interp1d(
        ref_pct, sorted_property, kind="linear",
        bounds_error=False,
        fill_value=(sorted_property[0], sorted_property[-1])
    )
    raw = np.maximum(f_interp(target_pct), 0)
    scale = total_amount / raw.sum() if raw.sum() > 0 else 0
    result_df.loc[mask_target, "property_attributed_income_s3"] = raw * scale
    return result_df

amount = df_attributed_income.loc[4, year_int] * 1000000
df = create_property_attributed_income(df, amount)
df["property_attributed_income_s3"] = df["property_attributed_income_s3"].fillna(0)

Strategy 4.name: labor_premium
Strategy 4.definition: Workplace labor income premium — firm fixed effect from regressing log labor income on workplace, age category, gender, nationality, and education
Strategy 4.logic: Population: ages 20–67 with positive labor income, at firms with ≥5 workers. Compute ln(labor_income_s4), define age categories (20–35, 36–50, 51–67), then run reghdfe ln_labor, absorb(labor_fe = firm_id, i.age_cat, i.male, i.jew, i.ba). The saved firm fixed effect is the labor premium, deduplicated at the firm level.
Strategy 4.code:
* Create log labor income (skip negatives/zeros)
gen ln_labor = ln($labor_income) if $labor_income > 0

* Filter to working age (20-67)
keep if $age >= 20 & $age <= 67

* Create age categories
gen age_cat = .
replace age_cat = 1 if $age >= 20 & $age <= 35
replace age_cat = 2 if $age >= 36 & $age <= 50
replace age_cat = 3 if $age >= 51 & $age <= 67

* Keep only observations with valid labor income
keep if !missing(ln_labor)

* Keep firms with >= 5 workers
bys firm_id: gen firm_n_l = _N
keep if firm_n_l >= 5

* Run regression with workplace fixed effects
reghdfe ln_labor, absorb(labor_fe = $workplace_id i.age_cat i.$male i.$jew i.$ba)

* Save workplace premiums (deduplicate at firm level)
keep $workplace_id labor_fe
duplicates drop $workplace_id, force
rename labor_fe labor_premium

Strategy 4.name: capital_premium
Strategy 4.definition: Workplace capital income premium — firm fixed effect from regressing log capital income on workplace, age category, gender, nationality, and education
Strategy 4.logic: Population: ages 20–67 with positive capital income, at firms with ≥5 workers. Compute ln(capital_income_5_s4), define age categories (20–35, 36–50, 51–67), then run reghdfe ln_capital, absorb(capital_fe = firm_id, i.age_cat, i.male, i.jew, i.ba). The saved firm fixed effect is the capital premium, deduplicated at the firm level.
Strategy 4.code:
* Create log capital income (skip negatives/zeros)
gen ln_capital = ln($capital_income) if $capital_income > 0

* Filter to working age (20-67)
keep if $age >= 20 & $age <= 67

* Create age categories
gen age_cat = .
replace age_cat = 1 if $age >= 20 & $age <= 35
replace age_cat = 2 if $age >= 36 & $age <= 50
replace age_cat = 3 if $age >= 51 & $age <= 67

* Keep only observations with valid capital income
keep if !missing(ln_capital)

* Keep firms with >= 5 workers
bys firm_id: gen firm_n_c = _N
keep if firm_n_c >= 5

* Run regression with workplace fixed effects
reghdfe ln_capital, absorb(capital_fe = $workplace_id i.age_cat i.$male i.$jew i.$ba)

* Save workplace premiums (deduplicate at firm level)
keep $workplace_id capital_fe
duplicates drop $workplace_id, force
rename capital_fe capital_premium

Production Classes.name: large_proprietor_first_def

Production Classes.logic: For each person-year (2006–2021), flag has_capital_income = 1 if HahnasaHonitAheret > 0 or HahnasaHonit > 0 or HahnasaLeLoYilshit > 0 (NaN filled with 0). Production Classes.name: manager
Production Classes.definition: Wage workers who are also managers (SugTikShatMas == 30 in tbl_source AND present in tbl_misrot)
Production Classes.logic: Sub-class of wage_and_salary_worker. Manager has priority over all other sub-classes. Set to 1 if the individual appears in tbl_source with SugTikShatMas == 30 AND appears in tbl_misrot in the same year. Period: 2006–2021.
Production Classes.code:
source_managers = tbl_source[
    (tbl_source['MisparTeudatZehut_fic'].notna()) &
    (tbl_source['SugTikShatMas'] == 30)
][['MisparTeudatZehut_fic', 'shana']].drop_duplicates()

    source_managers,
    on=['MisparTeudatZehut_fic', 'shana'],
    how='left'
)

    wage_sub['in_source_as_manager'].fillna(0).astype(int)

Production Classes.name: gig
Production Classes.definition: Wage workers in nonstandard employment (daily job, daily/hourly worker, or short tenure with long attachment)
Production Classes.logic: Sub-class of wage_and_salary_worker. Not a manager. Set to 1 if prat_sug_misra == 3 (daily job), or sug_oved in [2, 3] (before 2019), or worked_month <= 5 and tenure >= 2. Period: 2006–2021.
Production Classes.code:
cond_job_type = wage_sub['prat_sug_misra'].isin([3])

cond_worker_type = (
    (wage_sub['shana'] < 2019) &
    (wage_sub['sug_oved'].isin([2, 3]))
)

cond_low_months_high_tenure = (
    (wage_sub['worked_month'] <= 5) &
    (wage_sub['tenure'] >= 2)
)

wage_sub['gig'] = (
    (wage_sub['manager'] != 1) &
    (cond_job_type | cond_worker_type
     | cond_low_months_high_tenure)
).astype(int)

Production Classes.name: standard
Production Classes.definition: Wage workers in standard employment (sole monthly job, 10+ months worked, firm size 20+)
Production Classes.logic: Sub-class of wage_and_salary_worker. Not a manager or gig. Set to 1 if prat_sug_misra == 1 (sole job) and sug_oved == 1 (monthly worker) and worked_month >= 10 and firm_size >= 20. Period: 2006–2021.

Production Classes.name: gig_standard_mix
Production Classes.definition: Wage workers who are neither manager, gig, nor standard (residual sub-class)
Production Classes.logic: Sub-class of wage_and_salary_worker. Set to 1 if wage_and_salary_worker == 1 and manager != 1 and gig != 1 and standard != 1. Period: 2006–2021.

Production Classes.name: out_of_earned_income
Production Classes.definition: Individuals not classified into any other production class (residual category in merge)
Production Classes.logic: Not a separately computed indicator. In the merge, every person-year starts as 'Out of earned income population' and is overwritten if any class indicator is 1. Those with no indicator remain in this residual category.
Production Classes.code:
# Default — everyone starts here
merged['production_class'] = (
    'Out of earned income population'
)
# Then higher-priority classes overwrite
# in reverse priority order (see production_class)

Production Classes.name: production_class
Production Classes.definition: Final mutually exclusive production class label assigned by priority
Production Classes.logic: Priority-based assignment (highest wins): 1) Large Proprietor, 2) Small Proprietor, 3) Independent Producer – Formal / Informal / Unknown Education, 4) Wage & Independent Producer, 5) Wage Worker – Manager / Gig / Standard / Gig-Standard Mix, 6) Out of earned income population. Applied in reverse order so higher priority overwrites lower.
Production Classes.code:
merged['production_class'] = 'Out of earned income population'

# 5d  Gig/Standard Mix
mask = (merged['wage_and_salary_worker'] == 1) & (merged['gig_standard_mix'] == 1)
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Wage Worker – Gig/Standard Mix'

# 5c  Standard
mask = (merged['wage_and_salary_worker'] == 1) & (merged['standard'] == 1)
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Wage Worker – Standard'

# 5b  Gig
mask = (merged['wage_and_salary_worker'] == 1) & (merged['gig'] == 1)
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Wage Worker – Gig'

# 5a  Manager
mask = (merged['wage_and_salary_worker'] == 1) & (merged['manager'] == 1)
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Wage Worker – Manager'

# 4   Wage & Independent Producer
mask = merged['wage_and_independent_producer'] == 1
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Wage & Independent Producer'

# 3c  Independent Producer – Unknown Education
mask = (
    (merged['independent_producer'] == 1) &
    (merged['formal_independent_producer'] == 0) &
    (merged['informal_independent_producer'] == 0)
)
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Independent Producer – Unknown Education'

# 3b  Informal
mask = merged['informal_independent_producer'] == 1
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Independent Producer – Informal'

# 3a  Formal
mask = merged['formal_independent_producer'] == 1
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Independent Producer – Formal'

# 2   Small Proprietor
mask = merged['small_proprietor'] == 1
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Small Proprietor'

# 1   Large Proprietor
mask = merged['large_proprietor_first_def'] == 1
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Large Proprietor'

Production Classes.name: production_class_code
Production Classes.definition: Numeric code (1–11) corresponding to the production_class label
Production Classes.logic: Maps production_class text to a numeric code: 1 = Large Proprietor, 2 = Small Proprietor, 3 = Independent Producer – Formal, 4 = Independent Producer – Informal, 5 = Independent Producer – Unknown Education, 6 = Wage & Independent Producer, 7 = Wage Worker – Manager, 8 = Wage Worker – Gig, 9 = Wage Worker – Standard, 10 = Wage Worker – Gig/Standard Mix, 11 = Out of earned income population.
Production Classes.code:
class_codes = {
    'Large Proprietor':                        1,
    'Small Proprietor':                        2,
    'Independent Producer – Formal':           3,
    'Independent Producer – Informal':         4,
    'Independent Producer – Unknown Education': 5,
    'Wage & Independent Producer':             6,
    'Wage Worker – Manager':                   7,
    'Wage Worker – Gig':                       8,
    'Wage Worker – Standard':                  9,
    'Wage Worker – Gig/Standard Mix':         10,
    'Out of earned income population':        11
}

merged['production_class_code'] = (
    merged['production_class'].map(class_codes)
)

Production Classes.logic: Maps production_class to a broader grouping: Independent Producer sub-classes → 'Independent Producer'; Wage Worker sub-classes → 'Wage & Salary Worker'; all others unchanged.

Social Groups.logic: Computed via np.select: 1 = male Jews (min==1 & jew==1), 2 = female Jews (min==0 & jew==1), 3 = male Arabs (min==1 & jew==0), 4 = female Arabs (min==0 & jew==0). Default NaN.

Social Groups.name: generation
Social Groups.definition: Immigration generation classification (1 = 1st gen, 2 = 2nd gen, 2.5 = 2.5 gen, 3 = 3rd gen, 4 = 4th gen)
Social Groups.logic: Computed via np.select with 5 conditions: (1) 1st gen — born outside Israel (EretzLeida in Africa/America/Asia/Europe/Oceania); (2) 2nd gen — born in Israel, both parents born outside Israel; (2.5) — born in Israel, one parent born in Israel, other born abroad; (3) 3rd gen — self and both parents born in Israel, at least one grandparent not born in Israel (all grandparent fields non-null); (4) 4th gen — self, parents, and all grandparents born in Israel. Default NaN (~16% missing, concentrated among Arabs).
Social Groups.code:
conditions = [
    # First generation: born outside of Israel
    (Tbl_x2022['EretzLeida'].isin(['Africa', 'America', 'Asia', 'Europe', 'Oceania'])),

    # Second Generation: born in Israel, parents born outside Israel
    ((Tbl_x2022['EretzLeida'] == 'Israel') &
    (Tbl_x2022[['EretzLeidaAv', 'EretzLeidaEm']].isin(['Africa', 'America', 'Asia', 'Europe']).all(axis=1))),

    # Generation 2.5: one parent born in Israel, other born outside
    ((Tbl_x2022['EretzLeida'] == 'Israel') &
    (((Tbl_x2022['EretzLeidaAv'] == 'Israel') & (Tbl_x2022['EretzLeidaEm'].isin(['Africa', 'America', 'Asia', 'Europe']))) |
    ((Tbl_x2022['EretzLeidaEm'] == 'Israel') & (Tbl_x2022['EretzLeidaAv'].isin(['Africa', 'America', 'Asia', 'Europe']))))),

    # Third generation: both parents born in Israel, not all grandparents
    ((Tbl_x2022['EretzLeida'] == 'Israel') &
    (Tbl_x2022[['EretzLeidaAv', 'EretzLeidaEm']].isin(['Israel']).all(axis=1)) &
    (Tbl_x2022[['EretzLeidaAvAV', 'EretzLeidaAvEm', 'EretzLeidaEmAV', 'EretzLeidaEmEm']].notna().all(axis=1)) &
    ((~Tbl_x2022[['EretzLeidaAvAV', 'EretzLeidaAvEm', 'EretzLeidaEmAV', 'EretzLeidaEmEm']].isin(['Israel']).all(axis=1)))),

    # Fourth generation: all born in Israel
    ((Tbl_x2022['EretzLeida'] == 'Israel') &
    ((Tbl_x2022[['EretzLeidaAv', 'EretzLeidaEm', 'EretzLeidaAvAV', 'EretzLeidaEmAV', 'EretzLeidaAvEm', 'EretzLeidaEmEm']].isin(['Israel']).all(axis=1))))
]

values = [1, 2, 2.5, 3, 4]

Tbl_x2022['generation'] = np.select(conditions, values, default=np.nan)

)

mizrahi_father_ahkenazi_mother = (
    (Tbl_x2022['EretzLeidaAv'].isin(['Africa', 'Asia'])) &
    (Tbl_x2022['EretzLeidaEm'].isin(['America', 'Europe']))
)

Tbl_x2022['secondgmix'] = (
    second_generation &
    (ahkenazi_father_mizrahi_mother | mizrahi_father_ahkenazi_mother)
).astype(int)

Social Groups.name: secondhgmiz
Social Groups.definition: 2.5 generation Mizrahi (1 if foreign-born parent is Mizrahi and Israeli-born parent's parents are also Mizrahi or Israeli-born, NaN otherwise)
Social Groups.logic: 1 if generation==2.5 and the foreign-born parent is from Africa/Asia, and the Israeli-born parent's parents are also Mizrahi or Israeli-born. NaN otherwise.

mizrahi_father = (Tbl_x2022['EretzLeidaAv'].isin(['Africa', 'Asia']))
israeli_born_mother = (Tbl_x2022['EretzLeidaEm'] == 'Israel')
mother_side_at_least_one_grandparent_israli_born = (
    Tbl_x2022[['EretzLeidaAvEm', 'EretzLeidaEmEm']].isin(['Israel']).any(axis=1)
)
mother_side_all_grandparents_mizrahi = (
    Tbl_x2022[['EretzLeidaAvEm', 'EretzLeidaEmEm']].isin(['Africa', 'Asia']).all(axis=1)
)

mizrahi_mother = (Tbl_x2022['EretzLeidaEm'].isin(['Africa', 'Asia']))
israeli_born_father = (Tbl_x2022['EretzLeidaAv'] == 'Israel')
father_side_at_least_one_grandparent_israli_born = (
    Tbl_x2022[['EretzLeidaAvAV', 'EretzLeidaEmAV']].isin(['Israel']).any(axis=1)
)
father_side_all_grandparents_mizrahi = (
    Tbl_x2022[['EretzLeidaAvAV', 'EretzLeidaEmAV']].isin(['Africa', 'Asia']).all(axis=1)
)

mizrahi_father_israeli_born_mother = (mizrahi_father & israeli_born_mother)
mother_side_ok = (mother_side_all_grandparents_mizrahi | mother_side_at_least_one_grandparent_israli_born)

mizrahi_mother_israeli_born_father = (mizrahi_mother & israeli_born_father)
father_side_ok = (father_side_all_grandparents_mizrahi | father_side_at_least_one_grandparent_israli_born)

conditions_mizrahi = (
    second_and_a_half_generation &
    (
        (mizrahi_father_israeli_born_mother & mother_side_ok) |
        (mizrahi_mother_israeli_born_father & father_side_ok)
    )
)

Tbl_x2022['secondhgmiz'] = np.where(conditions_mizrahi, 1, np.nan)

Social Groups.logic: 1 if generation==2.5 and the foreign-born parent is from Europe/America, and the Israeli-born parent's parents are also Ashkenazi or Israeli-born. NaN otherwise.
Social Groups.code:

Social Groups.name: SemelYishuvnew2020
Social Groups.definition: CBS locality code (2020 classification)
Social Groups.logic: CBS source variable from tbl_yishuvomoeza95_2022. Identifies the locality (yishuv) of each individual using the 2020 CBS locality coding scheme. Used as a merge key to bring in locality-level attributes such as the socio-economic cluster.
Social Groups.code: # CBS source variable — used directly from tbl_yishuvomoeza95_2022

Social Groups.name: Moeza2020
Social Groups.definition: CBS regional council code (2020 classification)
Social Groups.logic: CBS source variable from tbl_yishuvomoeza95_2022. Identifies the regional council (moeza) of each individual using the 2020 CBS regional council coding scheme. Used as a merge key and as a fallback for the socio-economic cluster when the locality-level cluster is missing.
Social Groups.code: # CBS source variable — used directly from tbl_yishuvomoeza95_2022

Social Groups.name: cluster_yishuv_moeza
Social Groups.definition: Socio-economic cluster of the individual's locality, with regional council fallback
Social Groups.logic: Socio-economic cluster assigned to each individual. Uses the locality-level cluster (from yishuv_2020 lookup on SemelYishuvnew2020) when available. When the locality cluster is missing, falls back to the regional council-level cluster (from moeza_2020 lookup on Moeza2020, aggregated by mode). The moeza_2020 lookup is aggregated by Moeza2020 using the mode of cluster_moeza to handle duplicates.
Social Groups.code:
df_merge = pd.merge(tbl_yishuvomoeza95_2022,
    yishuv_2020[['yishuv_name', 'SemelYishuvnew2020', 'cluster']],
    on='SemelYishuvnew2020', how='left')

moeza_agg = moeza_2020[['regional_council_name', 'Moeza2020', 'cluster_moeza']]\
    .groupby('Moeza2020').agg({
        'regional_council_name': 'first',
        'cluster_moeza': pd.Series.mode
    }).reset_index()

df_merge = pd.merge(df_merge, moeza_agg, on='Moeza2020', how='left')

df_merge['cluster_yishuv_moeza'] = np.where(
    df_merge['cluster'].notna(),
    df_merge['cluster'],
    df_merge['cluster_moeza']
)
